concept: Past / Time Gone By / What Has Already Happened
description: The time before the present; events that have already occurred. 'Luera' names time that has flowed away—memory's domain, history's realm, what cannot be changed.
sound_symbolism: The word flows backward. 'Lu' begins with receding flow, time moving away. The vowel 'e' opens to memory. Then 'ra' reaches back, the past recalled.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mia luera halemu' (I remember the past). Opposite of 'wireo' (future). Related to the tense particle 'to' which marks past time.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The past exists only in present memory—clinging to it causes suffering, learning from it brings wisdom.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The past taught through memory—ancestral knowledge, seasonal patterns, accumulated experience.
